suspected_commits_reviewed.summary: Floating WebGL-glass control bar behind top-left gear; tabs reclaim vacated space
Wire tabs to horizontal lazy-loaded pages with click-to-swipe

root_causes.cause: The tab paging commit left `.workspace-page-surface` with permanent `will-change: transform, opacity`; Chromium treated it as a fixed-position containing block for drag ghosts at rest.
root_causes.fix: Keep `will-change: transform` only on active slide-in/slide-out page states; resting pages use `will-change: opacity`.

root_causes.cause: A fixed-position widget dragged from inside `.db-panel` resolved against the panel compositing context rather than the viewport.
root_causes.fix: Calibrate the active drag visual origin after the drag class is applied and subtract that origin from subsequent fixed `left/top` writes. The pointer-to-grid math remains in viewport coordinates.

root_causes.cause: Drag preview target rows could move past the committed bottom row, allowing the layout surface to expand from a drag.
root_causes.fix: Snapshot the committed bottom row at drag start and clamp preview/drop cells so drag cannot extend the committed grid extent.

intentional_non_changes: No collision/reflow scheduling or committed drop semantics were changed.
No Electron dependency or runtime dependency changed.
